solution: 
```cpp
#include <unordered_map>
#include <list>
#include <vector>

class LFUCache {
private:
    struct CacheNode {
        int key;
        int value;
        int freq;
        CacheNode(int k, int v) : key(k), value(v), freq(1) {}
    };

    int capacity;
    int size;
    int minFreq;
    std::unordered_map<int, std::list<CacheNode>> freqMap;
    std::unordered_map<int, std::list<CacheNode>::iterator> keyMap;

    void updateNode(std::list<CacheNode>::iterator& nodeIter) {
        int key = nodeIter->key;
        int value = nodeIter->value;
        int freq = nodeIter->freq;

        freqMap[freq].erase(nodeIter);
        if (freqMap[freq].empty()) {
            freqMap.erase(freq);
            if (minFreq == freq) minFreq++;
        }

        freqMap[freq + 1].push_front(CacheNode(key, value));
        nodeIter = freqMap[freq + 1].begin();
        nodeIter->freq = freq + 1;
    }

public:
    LFUCache(int capacity) : capacity(capacity), size(0), minFreq(0) {}

    int get(int key) {
        if (keyMap.find(key) == keyMap.end()) return -1;

        auto nodeIter = keyMap[key];
        int value = nodeIter->value;
        updateNode(nodeIter);
        return value;
    }

    void put(int key, int value) {
        if (capacity == 0) return;

        if (keyMap.find(key) != keyMap.end()) {
            auto nodeIter = keyMap[key];
            nodeIter->value = value;
            updateNode(nodeIter);
        } else {
            if (size == capacity) {
                auto& lfuList = freqMap[minFreq];
                int lfuKey = lfuList.back().key;
                lfuList.pop_back();
                if (lfuList.empty()) freqMap.erase(minFreq);
                keyMap.erase(lfuKey);
                size--;
            }

            freqMap[1].push_front(CacheNode(key, value));
            keyMap[key] = freqMap[1].begin();
            minFreq = 1;
            size++;
        }
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(N)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: This solution uses a combination of hash maps and doubly linked lists to maintain the LFU cache efficiently. The `keyMap` stores the mapping from keys to their corresponding nodes in the frequency map. The `freqMap` stores the mapping from frequencies to lists of nodes with that frequency. When a node is accessed, it is moved to the appropriate frequency list. If the cache is full, the least frequently used (and least recently used in case of ties) node is evicted. This ensures that both `get` and `put` operations run in O(1) average time complexity.
